title: Asyncronous reset not being triggered
body:
Hi,

Having issues getting verilator to trigger the async reset on flipflop, depending the initial state of the DUT.
This is fine if the async reset comes from the outside, because i can toggle it to ensure verilator sees an "edge".
But this is an issue when the async reset is driven by a flip flop in the design (reset controller with async assert / sync deassert)

Here is how to reproduce : 
 [example-systemverilog.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22494832/example-systemverilog.zip)

```
make
```
=> 
```
-- RUN ---------------------
obj_dir/Vtop
flipflop value 96
flipflop value after reset 42
<3 Success <3
flipflop value 97
flipflop value after reset 97
!!! Bad value flipflop reset :( !!!
make: *** [Makefile:91: run] Error 1
```

To explaine the design being tested it is : 
external async reset -> reset controller which async assert / sync deassert its output -> flip flop with async reset behaviour

And the testbench emit a reset pulse on the external async reset, and check that the flip flop got reseted properly.

Tested on linux Mint with verilator compiled from its master branch
--x-initial-edge, while fix this issue, bring a whole set of other problems, (--x-initial-edge isn't a viable workaround), see https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/4424

Regards
Charles
code_blocks:
make
-- RUN ---------------------
obj_dir/Vtop
flipflop value 96
flipflop value after reset 42
<3 Success <3
flipflop value 97
flipflop value after reset 97
!!! Bad value flipflop reset :( !!!
make: *** [Makefile:91: run] Error 1
